---
title: "LRP156: GP knee-test — receptive vocabulary (R) -> word reading (W)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP156 is a **GP knee-test**: a nonparametric (**HSGP**) re-attempt of the LRP56 mechanism, built
to test whether the receptive-vocabulary -> word-reading association has a **"knee"** — a level of
receptive vocabulary beyond which it is associated with a more marked difference in word reading —
the way [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058) found for letter sounds.

LRP56 itself was forced to a **linear** mechanism because the HSGP curve diverged (~300
divergences, unmoved by `target_accept` 0.99), so its estimand is a single slope, not a shape, and
no knee could be looked for. This variant switches the HSGP curve **back on** and lifts
`target_accept` to **0.999** (the setting that stabilises the letter-sound curve in LRP58) so the
curve gets a genuine chance to converge.

::: {.callout-important title="Non-convergence is itself the result"}
If the curve still diverges at `target_accept` 0.999, that **non-convergence is the honest result**
and the knee stays untestable for this exposure. Check the gate badge above before reading any
shape into the curve.
:::

## Adjustment set

Revised DAG (2026-07-10), re-derived by a backdoor d-separation search with the latent general
ability ($GA$) held — the criterion that reproduces LRP56/58 and dose-077 exactly. $R$'s observed
confounders that also reach word reading are $\{A, HS, TR, RW\}$; group $G\,(= IG)$ is the always-in
precision term and $W_{\text{pre}}$ the autoregressive baseline. This is **identical to LRP56** —
only the mechanism's functional form (nonparametric curve vs single slope) changes.

::: {.callout-warning title="Adjusted association, not causal"}
Residual confounding by latent general ability ($GA$) remains, so $f^R$ is an **adjusted
association**, not a causal effect. The randomised causal claim lives in the ITT suite.
:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
